intent: NEGATIVE FIXTURE, TWO DECLINE SIGNALS STACKED: a denial that is BOTH permissive=1 (did not actually block) AND an MCS constraint violation (s0:c0 vs s0:c5). floor=Permissive (report-not-suggest wins at the floor); authoritative=Constraint (audit2why ignores the permissive flag and replays the policy). Either way the answer is DECLINE.
_kinds_note: floor=Permissive: permissive=1 takes precedence at the floor (report-not-suggest, f4 contract #6) - the denial did not block, so an allow may grant access only seen because the domain was permissive. (The s0:c0 vs s0:c5 level mismatch would ALSO drive MlsSuspected, but Permissive is the dominant/most-conservative floor label.) authoritative=Constraint: audit2why IGNORES the permissive bit entirely (it re-derives the category by libsepol replay; f4 section 8) and reports the mlsconstrain. Both signals point to DECLINE.

oracle.audit2allow_N: allow container_t container_file_t:file write;  (BOGUS on both counts - ignores the permissive flag AND the constraint)
oracle.audit2allow_R: same BOGUS allow plus the constraint comment block
oracle.narrower_than_R_by: N/A - decline scenario. expected.te is rule-free and compiles exit 0.
oracle.audit2why_detail: mlsconstrain file { write setattr append unlink link rename } ((h1 dom h2 -Fail-) or (t1 != mcs_constrained_type -Fail-)); Constraint DENIED. Possible cause is the source level (s0:c0) and target level (s0:c5) are different. (audit2why output is byte-identical whether permissive=0 or 1.)

triage_expectation.should_decline_with_reason: Permissive denial (did not block) AND an MCS constraint violation (source category {c0} does not dominate target {c5}). Reported informational, NOT auto-allowed; and a TE allow cannot fix the constraint anyway.
triage_expectation.suspected_trap: A naive impl might (a) auto-allow the permissive denial (contract #6 violation), or (b) emit the BOGUS allow for the constraint, or both. The floor must apply Permissive precedence (report-not-suggest); the authoritative layer independently confirms Constraint. Both paths decline; an impl that suggests an allow fails twice over.
